title: PWA and browser notification client
objective: Add PWA installability, service worker scaffold and browser notification UI for an open portal tab or PWA window without third-party Web Push.
write_scope: static/service-worker.js or Django route for root-scoped service worker
non_goals: Do not register Push API subscriptions.
Do not request notification permission on page load.
Do not show marketing-style install prompts.
Do not rely on browser notification as the only visible notification path.
requirements: The user must first see an in-app explanation before the browser permission prompt.
The notification center must work without browser permission.
Polling must use the cursor API and back off on repeated failures.
System notifications must include safe title, body and target URL.
Service worker cache rules must not serve stale authenticated pages.
PWA install metadata must use project branding and proper icons.
acceptance_checks: The notification center appears in the portal shell.
Browser permission prompt is triggered only after a user click.
Denied permission leaves the notification center usable.
An incoming server notification appears in the center and, if allowed, as a system notification.
Clicking the notification opens the portal URL.
A closed browser is documented as unsupported for system notifications in stage 1.
